##NAMES=Riosecoite
##RRUFFID=R250054
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Ca_2_Mg(AsO_3_OH)_3_(H_2_O)_2_
##LOCALITY=Torrecillas mine, Iquique Province, Chile
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Colourless prisms intergrown in subparallel bundles in association with  camanchacaite (R250031), chinchorroite (R250032),  espadaite (R250051),  magnesiofluckite (R250052) and picaite (R250053). Type sample described in Kampf et al (2019)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250054
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Ca_1.97_Mg_0.97_Na_0.01_)[AsO_2.96_(OH)_1.04_]_3_(H_2_O)_2_
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 6.811 b: 7.3156 c: 11.7773 alpha: 83.466 beta: 84.394 gamma: 79.779 volume: 571.95 crystal system: triclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
